The authors evaluate their approach on a range of RL algorithms and classic control environments, and show that SEs and RNs can train agents more efficiently and effectively, and transfer to unseen agents.

reviews.summary_of_the_paper: The paper proposes a novel phase-oriented algorithm named PhaseFool that efficiently generates imperceptible audio adversarial examples with energy dissipation. PhaseFool leverages the spectrogram consistency of short-time Fourier transform (STFT) to adversarially transfer phase perturbations to the adjacent frames of magnitude spectrogram and dissipate the energy patterns in the spectrogram. The authors propose a weighted loss function to improve the imperceptibility of PhaseFool. Experimental results demonstrate that PhaseFool can generate full-sentence imperceptible audio adversarial examples and achieve a 6.64x generation speed-up over current state-of-the-art imperceptible counterparts.

reviews.summary_of_the_paper: The paper proposes a label trick that enables the parallel use of node features and labels for graph neural networks (GNNs). The authors prove that under certain assumptions, the stochastic label trick can be reduced to a deterministic training objective composed of two terms: a data-fitting term and a regularization factor conditioned on graph structure. They also introduce novel use cases for the label trick and provide experiments to verify its efficacy.
reviews.main_review: The paper provides a thorough analysis of the label trick, a widely adopted technique in GNNs. The authors derive a deterministic equivalent of the stochastic label trick and show that it can be viewed as a regularization factor that adapts to graph size and connectivity.
